ptr.theme: Crimson Scourge tier set — a complete miss
ptr.summary: Wowhead's Blood writer Mandl calls the Season 2 set a complete miss: it revolves almost solely around Crimson Scourge and Death and Decay, ignoring that CS can't proc while D&D is on the floor and that D&D sits at the very bottom of Blood's damage. Worse for a tank, it provides near-zero defensive value — "this feels like a DPS tier set."
changes: Set built entirely around Crimson Scourge, but CS can't proc while Death and Decay is a visible unit on the floor — the bonus fights the spec's own restrictions.
The 15% attack speed does little against Blood's ~3s swing timer, and Blood gains very little from extra auto-attacks anyway.
Doubling Death and Decay 'effectively does nothing' — at a meager 8.3% AP/second it's a footnote in the damage breakdown.
In AoE it raises the penalty for not fishing a proc (Heart Strike only cleaves 5 targets inside D&D) instead of increasing the payoff of playing properly.

PTR build 68569 (2026-07-08): the Venomous Abyss tier set is now implemented for testing with a NEW design — 2-set builds Blood Debt on Death Strike (Strength up to 5%, then a 10%-Strength Marrowrend at 10 stacks); 4-set makes that Marrowrend generate +3 Bone Shield and deal Shadow AoE. This is a distinct set from the Crimson Scourge / Death-and-Decay concept Mandl reviewed below.
ptr.set2: Moderate attack speed plus a small Crimson Scourge proc-chance bump — blunted by Blood's ~3s swings and the can't-proc-while-D&D-is-down restriction.
ptr.set4: Doubled Death and Decay damage plus 5% crit for 8s on consuming Crimson Scourge — doubling a bottom-of-the-list ability does nothing, and the crit is flat percentage, not rating, so Riposte converts none of it. Zero defensive value.
ptr.watch: Mandl's ask is a redesign — even tying the bonuses to Runes or RP spent "would feel like a breath of fresh air." As written, a defense-less DPS set gives a raid-B / M+-C tank nothing where it matters; watch for a rework before tuning has anything to carry. (Note: build 68569 replaced this Crimson Scourge/D&D set with a new Blood Debt / Marrowrend design that is now implemented for testing — Mandl's Negative verdict was of the earlier concept; the current set is pending fresh review.)

playstyle.notes: Combat rez (Raise Ally), Anti-Magic Shell/Zone, Death Grip + Gorefiend's Grasp; no raid buff, no Bloodlust
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins describes it as engaging but 'moderately complex' — RP-generation optimization plus chaining numerous small defensive cooldowns; AoEah rates it 'easy/forgiving.' Mid-complexity tank.

tierSet.set2: Your attack speed is increased by 15%, and Crimson Scourge's chance to occur is increased by an additional 5%.
tierSet.set4: Crimson Scourge increases the damage of your next Death and Decay by 100%, and grants 5% critical strike chance for 8 seconds when consumed.

playstyle.notes: Combat rez, Death Grip, Anti-Magic Zone, Blinding Sleet; guide rates utility 'Could Be Better', no raid buff
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: resource-management builder/spender with Killing Machine/Exterminate procs and burst windows, offers an Easy Mode; AoEah calls it 'a simple rotation of a builder and a spender... easy to understand, fun to pick up.'

tierSet.set2: Each time Remorseless Winter damages at least 1 enemy, gain Freezing Tempest which grants 2% attack speed and 4% increased Icy Death Torrent damage for 5 sec, stacking.
tierSet.set4: Remorseless Winter deals damage 25% more frequently, and Freezing Tempest lasts 5 seconds longer.

ptr.theme: Minion pruning + an all-passive tier set
ptr.summary: 12.1 cuts Unholy's active minion count (Army of the Dead rework, Reanimation becomes Lord of the Dead) and reshuffles burst damage, but Taeznak finds the execution half-baked: commanding your ghouls reduces to mindlessly spamming Scourge Strike, and the tier set is entirely passive. He likes the concepts — "I can see the intention, and it's incredible" — but says nearly every piece needs "a LOT of work."
changes: Army of the Dead no longer summons extra ghouls — you command 8 fixed ghouls via Death Order / Epidemic Order, picked by enemy count rather than the player; the net rotation is mindless Scourge Strike spam with Sudden Doom procs devalued.

Knock-on breakage: Doomed Bidding doesn't work at all (likely a bug), and Outnumber may fall from always-taken capstone to skipped outright, dragging lesser-ghoul talents (Infected Claws, Commander of the Dead, etc.) down with it.

Soul Reaper now consumes up to 3 Lesser Ghoul stacks instead of 2 Putrefy charges — forcing extra 2-rune Festering Strikes and courting resource starvation in execute, without fixing the old Commander-of-the-Dead overlap problem.

Army ghouls last 30s and the new Lord of the Dead lich concentrates damage back into burst windows — contradicting the stated burst-reduction goal; the messaging is 'confusing and contradictory' while sustained damage goes untouched.

PTR build 68569 (2026-07-08): overperformance nerf after first Heroic raid testing — all damage −8%, Lord of the Dead −25%, Magus of the Dead −15%, Lesser Ghoul damage −15%, Putrefy/Pestilence/Infected Claw/Virulent Plague −25%, Necrotic Coil −18%; Rider of the Apocalypse's Whitemane Death Coil/Undeath −25%.

Forum post #12 (2026-07-08, follow-up bug fix): Blizzard says the build-68569 tuning pass itself had a bug — the blanket all-damage reduction is corrected from −8% to −3%. Also fixed Nazgrim's Conquest incorrectly granting Strength when Magus of the Dead is sacrificed for Lord of the Dead (and from critters/low-level targets).
ptr.set2: Magi and Liches swap to a stronger Necrotic Bolt and Withering Grasp — "vaguely interesting" for spotlighting the new lich, but entirely passive with zero player control.
ptr.set4: +130% to both spells against enemies below 35% health — a flat execute amp Taeznak calls "about as bland and boring as it could possibly get"; the piece that "needs a LOT of work," and rare in being less interesting than the 2pc.
ptr.watch: Unholy enters 12.1 from an S1-topping baseline (M+ S-tier across boards), so a trim was expected — the complaint is direction, not numbers. Watch whether Blizzard picks a lane (sustained spec vs ~80%-of-damage-in-cooldowns), fixes the non-functional Doomed Bidding, and adds any decision-making to the one-button Army rotation before ship.

playstyle.notes: Combat rez, Death Grip, Anti-Magic Zone, Blinding Sleet; guide rates utility 'Lackluster', no raid buff
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: 'the rotation is simpler now with fewer opportunities for skill expression,' but requires plague/disease management, Dark Transformation/Army windows, and pet target-swapping. Moderate.

tierSet.set2: Your Magus of the Dead and Lord of the Dead cast a more powerful Necrotic Bolt instead of Frostbolt, and Withering Grasp instead of Shadow Bolt.
tierSet.set4: Necrotic Bolt's damage is increased by 130%, and Withering Grasp's damage is increased by 130%, against enemies below 35% health.

playstyle.notes: Chaos Brand raid buff, Darkness group defensive, Chaos Nova/Imprison/Sigil of Misery CC; no rez, no Bloodlust, no immunity
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: 'a lot of hidden complexity to optimize, but... very simple rules,' fast-paced with combo burst windows, has Easy Mode; AoEah rates it 'easy.' Accessible-moderate.

tierSet.set2: Blade Dance, Chaos Strike, and Essence Break deal 12% increased damage.
tierSet.set4: Essence Break now applies and benefits from the effects of Cycle of Hatred, has 25% increased initial strike damage, and has 2 seconds of increased duration.

ptr.theme: Void Meta flattened → Soulburst Reap set
ptr.summary: Blizzard flattens Devourer's all-in Void Meta profile — Mastery's in-Meta bonus cut 66%, offset by a rare 20% all-ability aura buff — and the author judges the stated goal achieved: slightly less damage inside the cooldown, significantly more outside it. The Soulburst tier set is "AWESOME" and rotationally transformative, turning Reap into a main focus; the author is having a LOT of fun on PTR.
changes: Mastery: Monster Within's Void Meta bonus cut 66% — the author's own 45% in-Meta mastery drops to 15% on identical gear — pushing gearing toward Crit/Haste come 12.1.
All ability damage +20% — a 'GIGANTIC' aura buff, rare at this size; with nothing else changing it still nets roughly a 10% loss for Annihilator, before retuning.
Impending Apocalypse star ramp 30% → 20% (a 5-star window's final star falls from +120% to +80%); Void Meta's Void Ray bonus 67% → 40%; Consume +60%.

The rebalance hits Annihilator harder than Void-Scarred — likely forcing a hero-talent swap — while Void-Scarred's no-synergy caster/melee split (and Devourer's Bite, which 'needs to go') is left unaddressed.
Demon Hunters can now equip daggers — fun access to the new cantrip daggers, otherwise uneventful.
PTR build 68569 (2026-07-08): all ability damage increased by 10% (an underperformance buff), while sibling spec Havoc took −5%.
ptr.set2: Soulburst: a 4+ soul Reap has a 20% chance (deck-of-cards system, so low variance) to make the next Consume instant and explode in AoE — only 1-2% overall, plus minor instant-cast QoL.
ptr.set4: Where the set shines: Soulburst generates 8 Soul Fragments plus Moment of Craving and Reap gains 20% damage — one of the kit's most efficient soul generators, "rotationally transformative," and a bigger win for Void-Scarred than Annihilator.
ptr.watch: Eradicate currently can't proc Soulburst — a significant AoE/cleave proc loss the author calls a bug that "needs to be fixed ASAP" — and Void-Scarred's caster/melee dysfunction went untouched. From today's S/A-tier baseline the author still expects a "great spot" at 12.1 launch, provided that fix lands.

playstyle.notes: Chaos Brand raid buff, Darkness, 30yd Disrupt interrupt, Void Nova ranged AoE stun; no rez, no Bloodlust, no immunity
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: 'smooth pick-up-and-play feel' with hidden Void Metamorphosis depth; AoEah rates it 'Easiest — designed for maximum simplicity,' simpler than Havoc with limited buttons. Simple with optional depth.

tierSet.set2: Harvesting 4 or more Soul Fragments with Reap has a 20% chance to cause your next Consume to be instant cast and explode in a Soulburst, dealing Cosmic damage to nearby enemies.
tierSet.set4: Soulburst generates 8 Soul Fragments and grants Moment of Craving. Reap deals 20% increased damage.

ptr.theme: Housekeeping + Sigil reshuffle
ptr.summary: Mostly housekeeping paired with a Sigil reshuffle that leaves Vengeance with arguably the least crowd-control it has ever had. The tier set brings no tanking value.
changes: Sigil layout reshuffled; net loss of control tools.
General numbers/housekeeping pass, no identity change.
No new defensive or throughput hook to change how it plays.
ptr.set2: Offensive-leaning bonus with no meaningful tank utility.
ptr.set4: Does nothing to address the raid case — effectively worthless as a tank set.
ptr.watch: Baseline is raid-C / M+-A — great in keys, benched in raid. The set does nothing to fix the raid-desirability problem, and Havoc/Devourer cover the DH utility a raid would want.

metrics.name: Median rDPS (12.1 PTR M+ testing, tank)

playstyle.notes: Chaos Brand raid buff, Sigil of Silence/Misery, Chaos Nova, Darkness; strong M+ control but 'nothing unique' in raids, no rez
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: build/spend soul cycle plus weaving multiple sigils and overlapping defensives for offense at a defensive cost; AoEah rates it 'Moderate — much smoother rotation.'

tierSet.set2: Sigil of Flame's damage over time is increased by 50%. Soul Cleave increases the duration of Sigil of Flame on your primary target by 2 seconds.
tierSet.set4: Immolation Aura and Sigil of Spite deal 100% increased damage against targets affected by your Sigil of Flame.

ptr.theme: Touch the Cosmos nerf
ptr.summary: The pass centers on reining in Touch the Cosmos, with Lunar Calling dropping from 120% to 100%. The tier set (Bark of the Enigmatic Dreamwatcher) reinforces the existing playstyle rather than reshaping it.
changes: Lunar Calling 120% → 100%.
Touch the Cosmos proc value pulled back — the central lever.
A tuning pass, not a redesign — how the spec plays is unchanged.

PTR build 68569 (2026-07-08): across-the-board buff — all damage +3.5%, Moonfire/Sunfire/Shooting Stars/Wrath +10%, Starfire +20%, Umbral Intensity 12%, plus Keeper of the Grove Starfall buffs (dev intent: bring DoT/builder damage up to match the strong Starsurge/Starfall spenders).
ptr.set2: Feeds the current Eclipse / Astral flow.
ptr.set4: Reinforces the existing rotation without adding a new decision.
ptr.watch: Net power shift is the whole story here; nothing changes about the moment-to-moment. Lukewarm.

playstyle.notes: Combat rez (Rebirth), Mark of the Wild, Innervate, Solar Beam interrupt, Cyclone/roots/Typhoon, Soothe; cast-heavy on the move
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: 'simple but rewarding rotation... with a level of depth,' Eclipse + Astral Power management; AoEah rates it 'Hard — rotation and setup are more involved.' Sources split, settling at moderate.

tierSet.set2: Starsurge damage increased by 20%. Starfall also deals Astral damage instantly to all valid enemies in range when cast.
tierSet.set4: Your damage is increased by 10% while in any Eclipse. This bonus wanes to 2% at the midpoint of the Eclipse, then waxes back to 10% at the end.

playstyle.notes: Combat rez (Rebirth), Mark of the Wild, Innervate, Stampeding Roar, Skull Bash, Typhoon/Ursol's Vortex CC
playstyle.complexityNotes: Wowhead/Method: 'intimidating... two resources and multiple debuffs to maintain,' with expected downtime as the first hurdle; Icy Veins notes Midnight 'significantly reduced management' and calls the slower pace 'intuitive.' Moderate.

tierSet.set2: When Berserk or Incarnation: Avatar of Ashamane ends, you deal 10% increased damage for 1 second for each combo point spent during Berserk or Incarnation: Avatar of Ashamane.
tierSet.set4: Berserk and Incarnation: Avatar of Ashamane last 10 seconds longer.

metrics.name: Median rDPS (12.1 PTR M+ testing, tank)

playstyle.notes: Combat rez (Rebirth), Mark of the Wild, Stampeding Roar, Dream of Cenarius off-heals, Incapacitating Roar; loaded for a tank
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins describes the playstyle as 'relatively straightforward' — rage builder/spender with Moonfire spread; AoEah rates it 'Easiest — set and forget... nearly unkillable.' Simple tank.

tierSet.set2: Thrash has a 20% chance to make your next Mangle deal 100% increased damage.
tierSet.set4: Thrash calls up 3 thorns, each impaling a target for Nature damage and dealing additional Nature damage to up to 5 nearby enemies, and extends the duration of Berserk by 0.5 seconds, up to 5 seconds.

playstyle.notes: Combat rez (Rebirth), Mark of the Wild ('most powerful raid buff'), Ironbark external, Cyclone/Typhoon/Ursol's Vortex CC, Nature's Cure dispel
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: flexible HoT kit that requires 'ramping' pre-planning and 'tracking many HoTs at once' (though Midnight trimmed some); AoEah rates it 'Easy — forgiving.' Moderate for the ramp/planning layer.

tierSet.set2: Rejuvenation has a 15% chance and Nature's Swiftness has a 100% chance to grant Genesis, causing all your heal over time effects to heal for 25% more for 8 seconds. Multiple applications may overlap.
tierSet.set4: Genesis duration is increased by 4 seconds, and gaining Genesis grants Clearcasting.

ptr.theme: Double-time capped, play-it-safe tier set
ptr.summary: The Chronowarden Double-time rework caps a critical Ebon Might's stat bonus at 15 seconds and makes it a trackable buff, fixing the spec's worst variance and tracking problems, while Apex Duplicate's Upheavals lose their knockup — "a strict improvement" for raids. The tier set is effective but uninspiring, and barring tuning Augmentation looks largely unchanged going into Season 2.
changes: Double-time capped: Ebon Might crit stats now last 15s (Mastery-scaled) as a trackable buff — no more single dice roll deciding a chunk of your damage.
Time Skip can still chain two crit Ebon Mights for up to 30s of Double-time — enough to cover a full opener burst window.
Apex Duplicate's Upheavals no longer knock enemies airborne — a strict raid improvement where add positioning matters, and fewer missed dungeon interrupts.
Healing abilities up 25% across the board (Living Flame, Verdant Embrace, Emerald Blossom, Panacea, Defy Fate, Molten Blood), in line with other DPS specs.

PTR build 68569 (2026-07-08): Scalecommander's Wingleader redesigned — per-target Deep Breath/Breath of Eons CDR replaces the old spread-Bombardment mechanic (Command Squadron Pyre +40%, Maneuverability DoT +40% to compensate); Chronowarden's Double-time now extends rather than overwrites on reapply. Devs say they'll keep watching Augmentation's performance.

Wingleader reaction (Wowhead Aug writer Jereico, 2026-07-09, news=382133): the redesign cuts ~70% of Scalecommander's average Breath of Eons CDR in AoE (up to 80% single-target); the Command Squadron/Maneuverability +40% buffs 'most certainly do not compensate'. He is not immediately worried about the damage (expects considerable numerical tuning before Season 2) but flags the gameplay-feel cost — Bombardments loses its engine role and Scalecommander risks playing nearly identically to Chronowarden.
ptr.set2: Upheaval CD −10s — straightforward, directly benefits both Chronowarden and Scalecommander.
ptr.set4: Upheaval magnifies Fate Mirror for 8s (echo 15% → 45%) — a bigger number on a ~3%-of-total-damage passive with no gameplay hook; the author doubts it changes how anyone plays.
ptr.watch: The S1 set is strong enough that a 2+2 setup "is actually looking quite attractive" — expect the old set to be tuned down at season start. From its raid-S/A, M+-staple baseline the article sees Aug "largely unchanged" into Season 2, with rough edges (Ebon Might phasing, dead Mastery/Vers) still unaddressed. Post-68569: watch Scalecommander tuning — Jereico calls the Wingleader nerf one of the most consequential Aug changes of Midnight and expects further adjustments before S2.

playstyle.notes: Bloodlust (Fury of the Aspects), Ebon Might/Prescience damage amp, Source of Magic, Rescue/Zephyr externals, Sleep Walk CC, Cauterizing Flame dispel
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: priority 'not very complicated as a whole'; Midnight brought 'a large simplification of buff management,' depth lives in Prescience/Ebon Might target selection where mistimed casts lose DPS — moderate.

tierSet.set2: Upheaval's cooldown is reduced by 10 seconds.
tierSet.set4: Upheaval magnifies your Fate Mirror effects for 8 seconds, increasing the amount of damage echoed to 45%.

ptr.theme: Apex redesign → Unbound Flame
ptr.summary: The Apex talent is redesigned into Unbound Flame (a sub-5% damage shift), leaving the rotation largely intact. The tier set is a genuine success, buffing Shattering Star passively.
changes: Apex reworked into Unbound Flame — under 5% of damage, low disruption.
Core rotation stays familiar; this is a refinement, not an overhaul.

PTR build 68569 (2026-07-08): Scalecommander's Wingleader redesigned — per-target Deep Breath/Breath of Eons CDR replaces the old spread-Bombardment mechanic, with Command Squadron's Pyre +40% and Maneuverability's DoT +40% as area-damage compensation.
ptr.set2: Clean, passive value.
ptr.set4: Buffs Shattering Star without adding button bloat — one of the better-designed sets.
ptr.watch: The set is good, but a raid-B / M+-C baseline means tuning still has to do the heavy lifting; a well-designed set alone won't move it.

playstyle.notes: Bloodlust (Fury of the Aspects), Source of Magic (3% healing + mana), Rescue, Zephyr, Blessing of the Bronze; weaker in dungeons
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: 'a good amount of depth in your combat rotation... a more involved and interesting DPS specialization,' built on empower/Essence management around Dragonrage — moderate.

tierSet.set2: Shattering Star's damage is increased by 150%, and it always casts as if you had reached maximum empower level.
tierSet.set4: Causality reduces the remaining cooldown of your empower spells by an additional 0.1 seconds each time the effect occurs. Eternity Surge deals 10% increased damage.

ptr.theme: Dream Breath rework
ptr.summary: Dream Breath is reworked for a net ~+10%, pushed into the HoT portion, and Flameshaper ascends as the hero tree of choice. The first clearly-positive verdict in the series, with a strong 2pc.
changes: Dream Breath net ~+10%, shifted toward the heal-over-time.
Flameshaper pulls ahead as the preferred hero tree.
Rewards the ramp/HoT playstyle Prevoker already leans on.
ptr.set2: Strong on its own — immediately felt.
ptr.set4: Complements the Dream Breath change and the Flameshaper direction.
ptr.watch: Baseline is raid-A / M+-B; the changes clearly help the raid profile most. The best-received set + kit pass of the tracked healers.

playstyle.notes: Bloodlust (Fury of the Aspects), Zephyr party DR, Rescue, Obsidian Scales external; no raid buff, guide calls utility its biggest weakness
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: Midnight goal was 'to make the spec much more approachable for new players while also removing some of the very tight timings' — previously demanding, now moderate empower/echo management.

tierSet.set2: Consuming Essence Burst sends forth a Living Flame at your target at 150% effectiveness, or at a nearby injured target if your primary target is at full health.
tierSet.set4: Green spells restore 5% more health, and Verdant Embrace has a 100% chance to grant Essence Burst.

ptr.theme: Power pulled out of Bestial Wrath into the baseline — flatter, more forgiving, same feel
ptr.summary: Azortharion reads 12.1 as a rebalance rather than a rework: power is shifted out of Bestial Wrath windows and cooldowns into baseline abilities, so the spec plays flatter and more evenly. He notes AoE takes a real hit, but the moment-to-moment gameplay feel is largely unchanged — if you enjoy BM now, you'll enjoy it post-patch, and vice versa.
changes: Piercing Fangs (crit damage in Bestial Wrath) significantly nerfed, and Bloody Frenzy nerfed — both pulling power out of the burst window; core abilities buffed to compensate
Kill Cleave now half as effective and Wild Instincts removed entirely, a substantial AoE nerf
Beast Cleave duration up to 10s, so you no longer have to be perfect with Wild Thrash on cooldown (though casting it immediately is still best)
Net effect: a flatter power profile spread more evenly across the rotation rather than concentrated in cooldowns

Wowhead's Tarlo corroborates the Mixed read: core buffs (Barbed Shot / Kill Command / Cobra Shot +20–25%, Beast Cleave to 10s, Hunter's Mark to 5 targets) are solid, but the Piercing Fangs redesign lands 'very bad' and BM still lacks a strong niche on big pulls or burst phases

PTR build 68569 (2026-07-08): Cobra Shot reshaped — base damage +30% and Auto Shot +20%, but its modifiers were cut (Serpentine Strikes crit-dmg 20% was 50%, Cobra Senses 10% was 35%, Pack Leader's Hogstrider +100% was 200%) and Barbed Shot −12%; the 4-set single-target bonus dropped to +15%/stack (was 30%). Devs continuing to monitor BM throughput.
ptr.set2: Reads as more of an AoE boost than a single-target one. Tarlo details it as Barbed Shot making pets Stomp one extra time at 50% effectiveness.
ptr.set4: Stomp grants a Cobra Shot benefit — 20% Beast Cleave effectiveness or a 30% single-target bonus (stacking to 4). Because the 4pc reads the cosmetic Beast Cleave buff, optimal play requires manually '/cancelaura Beast Cleave' in priority-AoE, 2-target, and burst windows — Tarlo flags this as an unfortunate degenerate interaction. Azortharion expects the set will mostly play itself otherwise.
ptr.watch: Watch the AoE ceiling: with Wild Instincts gone, Kill Cleave halved, and burst power flattened into the baseline, BM's multi-target output leans on the longer Beast Cleave and the tier set to hold up.

playstyle.notes: Bloodlust (pet Primal Rage), Hunter's Mark 3% buff, Turtle immunity, Tranquilizing Shot, Counter Shot, Intimidation/Binding Shot CC, Roar of Sacrifice external
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: 'simple rotation,' 'does not require you to weave in your cooldowns,' 'ideal for someone new to raiding or just WoW,' fully mobile — very simple/beginner-friendly.

tierSet.set2: Barbed Shot causes your pets to Stomp 1 additional time at 50% effectiveness.
tierSet.set4: Stomp causes your next Cobra Shot to either benefit from Beast Cleave at 20% effectiveness, or strike your primary target for 30% additional damage, stacking up to 4 times.

ptr.theme: The most reworked spec in the patch, riding on how Explosive Shot lands
ptr.summary: Azortharion calls 12.1 "a very mixed bag" for Marksmanship and admits he's "pretty conflicted" — MM sees the most gameplay change of any spec, but the outcome hinges entirely on the new Explosive Shot. He's positive on the Deathblow and Black Arrow fixes, but a heavy Bulletstorm nerf and reduced Aimed Shot emphasis leave the net direction genuinely uncertain: "Could be good, could be bad."
changes: Explosive Shot redesigned into a 3-second AoE DoT ticking every second (extendable to 5s), with talent branches to either double the cast for stacked damage or cut its cooldown from 30s to 20s — Azortharion 'likes the double cast part'
Black Arrow gets a 40% damage buff to offset the Withering Fire nerf and Headshot removal
Deathblow procs now guarantee the next Aimed Shot is empowered, removing rotational unpredictability — 'very good change'
Bulletstorm cut to +20% Aimed Shot damage (from 30%) per 10 stacks and now hits only the next shot instead of ~three — 'a massive nerf to the Talent and especially MM's funnel/prio damage'
Aimed Shot de-emphasized: crit-scaling replaced with a flat 10% crit-damage bump, and Focused Aim's cooldown reduction dropped from 2s to 1s, lowering Aimed Shot frequency

PTR build 68569 (2026-07-08): sizeable catch-up buffs — Multi-Shot +80%, Steady Shot +50%, Kill Shot +25%, Rapid Fire/Volley/Auto Shot +20%, Arcane Shot +15%, Dark Ranger's Black Arrow +25% (devs explicitly bringing MM's damage up after the patch's reworks).
ptr.set2: Reads as a straightforward Explosive Shot amplifier — adds 1s of duration for an extra tick, leaning into the ability the whole patch's success rides on.
ptr.set4: Grants cooldown reduction off Explosive Shot ticks, but he flags that it "will not scale with AoE" — so the payoff stays single-target-weighted rather than rewarding the DoT's new multi-target ceiling.
ptr.watch: With Bulletstorm gutted and Aimed Shot pushed to the background, MM's priority/funnel damage takes a real hit — whether the spec comes out ahead depends almost entirely on how the reworked Explosive Shot actually performs in practice.

playstyle.notes: Bloodlust (pet Primal Rage), Hunter's Mark 3% buff, Turtle immunity, Tranquilizing Shot, Counter Shot, Intimidation/Freezing Trap CC; ~40% time in immobile Aimed Shot casts
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: 'high potential, but high skill floor,' ~40% immobile casts, needs 'excellent awareness of Focus levels and cooldowns at all times' plus Trick Shots weaving under movement — high.

tierSet.set2: Explosive Shot lasts 1 second longer and deals 20% increased damage.
tierSet.set4: Aimed Shot and Rapid Fire damage increased by 5%. Each time Explosive Shot causes a burst of damage, the cooldown of Aimed Shot and Rapid Fire is reduced by 0.5 seconds.

ptr.theme: Primal Surge moved off capstone
ptr.summary: Primal Surge is relocated up out of the capstone slot, and a new Razor Edge node appears as a suspicious-looking 2-point filler. The tier set is fully passive and misses every stated Blizzard tier-set goal.
changes: Primal Surge moved up from the capstone position.
New Razor Edge node — reads like a 2-point tax rather than a real choice.
Kit changes don't add interactivity.
ptr.set2: Passive throughput, no gameplay hook.
ptr.set4: Also passive — misses all of Blizzard's own goals for the set (no rotational change, no new decision).
ptr.watch: Baseline is strong (raid-S), so this won't tank the spec — but the set is a wasted opportunity to make it more engaging.

playstyle.notes: Bloodlust (pet Primal Rage), Hunter's Mark 3% buff, Turtle immunity, Tranquilizing Shot, Muzzle interrupt, Freezing Trap/Intimidation CC, Harpoon gap-closer
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: rotation is 'very dynamic, fast-paced, and busy'; high skill ceiling, best players 'make the correct ability decision on the fly' amid competing mechanics — high.

tierSet.set2: Raptor Strike damage increased by 15%.
tierSet.set4: Mongoose Fury also increases the damage of Wildfire Bomb by 10%.

ptr.theme: New burst tool, defenses still short
ptr.summary: A new Apex talent (Prismatic Bolt) and tier set give Arcane a fresh AoE/burst tool, and Porom is glad Blizzard responded to feedback, but he still calls the spec "a shell of what it was less than a year ago" — defenses remain inadequate even after a new mitigation talent.
changes: New Apex talent Prismatic Bolt: replaces Arcane Blast when triggered by Arcane Barrage, AoE arcane damage, generates 4 Arcane Charges, proc chance scales 1-2% per rank — Porom flags it as currently tuned too strong.
Improved Prismatic Barrier: grants an additional charge + 5% magic damage reduction.
New talent Refractive Images: redirects 10% of damage over 8s, 30% while Mirror Image is active — welcome attempt but Porom says it 'is clearly not going to solve the problem.'
Arcane Pulse redesigned: cast time down to 2s, radius up to 8yd, 15s cooldown, generates 1 charge per enemy hit.
Losing Touch of the Archmage removed a reliable burst window, shifting the spec toward a reactive 'hit what lights up' playstyle with less player agency.

ptr.theme: From "zero changes" to a genuinely fun 4pc
ptr.summary: Clarius notes Holy Pally opened 12.1 with literally zero patch-note changes beyond a tier set, but the 6/30 build turned it around: the 4pc went from a coin-flip to a guaranteed Infusion of Light on Holy Light, and talent tuning to Eternal Flame and Pillars of Light opened up genuinely fresh, more fun builds. Good changes overall — he just wishes more of the talent-tree work had been baseline instead of tied to a temporary set.
changes: 4pc buffed on 6/30 from a 50% chance to a 100% chance for Holy Light to grant Infusion of Light — 'a great change,' you can now always rely on Holy Light for Infusions and stop spamming them back to back.

6/30 also brought Eternal Flame closer to Light of Dawn, opening builds that drop Unending Light / Breaking Dawn and enable Empyrean Legacy / Truth Prevails with EF as the main HP spender — better healing profile and more fun.
Pillars of Light + the EF buff + the new set make Beacon of Virtue far more appealing; for M+, where EF was already best, it's a straight buff.
2pc increases Infusion of Light additively: Infused Flash of Light heals +275% instead of +200% (a 25% bump) and Greater Judgment hits +225% instead of +150% (a 30% bump).

PTR build 68569 (2026-07-08): broad buff for talent diversity — all healing +8%, Holy Shock +10%, the 2-set now doubles Infusion of Light (100%, was 75%), plus buffs to Truth Prevails, Saved by the Light, Overflowing Light, Awakening, Ringing of the Heavens and more.
ptr.set2: A pretty standard set — makes Infusions feel better and more important not to waste (+25% on Infused Flash of Light, +30% on Greater Judgment), but not particularly strong compared to other healers' 2pc bonuses.
ptr.set4: Originally a 50% chance for Holy Light to grant Infusion of Light — which also caused wasted procs since you don't know you've proc'd until you've already queued the next spell. The 6/30 buff to a guaranteed 100% chance fixes that and is 'a great change': Holy Light is now a reliable Infusion generator, no more back-to-back spamming, and much more fun to play.
ptr.watch: The 4pc exists to fix real gameplay gaps — making Judgment worth casting without an Infusion proc and cutting hardcast Flash of Light — and Clarius argues effects that fix gameplay issues should be baseline, not gated behind a temporary tier set. He also hoped for more talent-tree cleanup: lots of weak kiss/curse talents (Call of the Righteous, Rising Sunlight, Seraphic Barrier), and the essential Inflorescence of the Sunwell is still buried behind three mediocre talents.

tierSet.set4: Judgment now has a 20% chance to grant Infusion of Light. Holy Light now has a 100% chance to grant Infusion of Light.

ptr.theme: Talent reshuffle and a damage-amp set, offset by quiet nerfs
ptr.summary: The Hammer of Wrath crew (Woliance, Fluttershy) read 12.1 as a mixed bag: a talent-tree reshuffle opens up new pairings (Sentinel now sits alongside Avenging Wrath) and a Crusader Strike tier set leans hard into damage, but a string of undocumented nerfs to Divine Exaction, Blessed Assurance and Glory of the Vanguard quietly claw offense back, with a bug list still pending fixes.
changes: Talent shuffle: Blessed Word (WoG crit) becomes a choice node with Valiant Crusade; Sentinel is now pickable together with Avenging Wrath (moved to the old Empyrean Authority spot), and Empyrean Authority slides into the old Sanctified Wrath slot.
Undocumented nerf: Divine Exaction cut to +50% extra damage, down from +150%.
Undocumented nerf: Blessed Assurance now +100% extra on Blessed Hammer / Hammer of the Righteous after a spender, down from +200%.
Undocumented nerf: Glory of the Vanguard secondary damage reduced to 50%, down from 100%.
Undocumented buff: Undying Embers now heals for at least 125% of Refining Fire damage, scaling up to 300% as HP drops.

PTR build 68569 (2026-07-08): Lightsmith's Divine Guidance redesigned (each Holy Power ability empowers the next Consecration to deal split Holy damage and heal up to 3 allies); several fixes/QoL (Blessed Word, Valiant Crusade no longer cancels on death, Improved Ardent Defender fatal-damage indicator) and Guardian of Ancient Kings' initial cooldown raised to 8s.
ptr.set2: The "Crusader Strike" set (Blessed Hammer, Hammer of the Righteous, Crusader Strike and Judgment) is a pure damage amp — those hits deal 20% increased damage, or 60% if the hit crits. The crew reads it as a straightforward offensive lever that rewards building around crit to hit the higher tier, doubling down on Prot's damage rotation rather than adding defensive value.
ptr.watch: Note: the sources describe the tier set as a single combined mechanic and do not cleanly split a 2pc vs 4pc bonus, so the whole tier-set read sits in set2 and set4 is left empty. These are PTR values on top of an early, still-shifting tree — several of the nerfs are undocumented (Fluttershy) and the Hammer of Wrath Discord is tracking a live bug list, so the 60%-on-crit set payoff and the net offensive floor could both move before 12.1 ships. Treat the numbers as against the 12.0.7 baseline.

ptr.theme: Smoother Holy Power, but a controversial 4-set that fights the spec's identity
ptr.summary: Bolas's Wowhead review. The 12.1 changes make Holy Power generation far more predictable and much faster — decoupling Ret from needing a Shaman's Skyfury and letting Art of War stack twice, both of which Bolas thinks feel genuinely better. But he argues the direction keeps pushing Ret toward "spend Holy Power ASAP with little agency," Avenging Wrath eats a notable burst nerf, and the real story is the tier set: a solid, inoffensive 2pc paired with a 4pc that's "one of the most controversial set bonuses in the Retribution community in recent memory" for forcing you to use the "wrong" spender for the target count.
changes: Crusading Strikes flips from −20% to +15% auto-attack speed and no longer procs off Skyfury or generates Holy Power from extra Skyfury attacks — roughly 20% more hits, decoupling Ret from needing a Shaman in group and smoothing (but flooding) Holy Power generation.

Art of War can now accumulate one extra charge (Apex Talent: Light Within Rank 1) — Bolas's favourite change; removes the awkward Holy Power loss from having to instantly spend procs. Side effect: makes Improved Blade of Justice much worse, since its use case was banking charges for procs.

Damage shuffled out of cooldowns into the core kit: Blade of Justice +40%; Templar Strike/Slash, Judgment, Hammer of Wrath all +50%; Final Verdict and Divine Storm +15%. Offsetting nerfs: Art of War's Blade of Justice bonus 150%→80%, Divine Toll's Judgment 100%→50%, Light Within Rank 3 damage −25%.

Avenging Wrath now grants +15% damage and crit (was +20%) — Bolas calls it a fairly severe nerf given Ret's AW window wasn't especially strong to begin with, though 12.1 is bringing cooldown nerfs to most specs.
Healing buffed across the board: Flash of Light, Word of Glory, and Eternal Flame all +25%. Templar's Hammer of Light now costs 3 Holy Power (was 5) but deals −30% damage (does not affect PvP).

PTR build 68569 (2026-07-08): all damage −6%, and the Venomous Abyss 4-set reworked — consuming Divine Purpose now grants Divine Arbiter (empowering other Holy Power spenders to hit for Holy AoE; Divine Arbiter damage +35%), an attempt to address the tier set's pain points while keeping its identity.
ptr.set2: Divine Purpose gains an additional 10% chance to activate, and consuming it grants Divine Power — +10% Holy damage done for 12 seconds. Bolas calls it "pretty solid and inoffensive": a clean, uncontroversial throughput bonus.
ptr.set4: Consuming Divine Purpose with Divine Storm grants 2 stacks of Divine Arbiter, making your next Final Verdicts unleash an AoE Holy nuke (8-yd splash) — but only if no Arbiter stacks are already active, so once you hold stacks you must spend Divine Purpose on Final Verdict (the single-target spender) instead. "One of the most controversial set bonuses in the Retribution community in recent memory": it forces the "wrong" spender for the target count, which the official feedback thread explicitly flagged as a degenerate/unintended rotation impact. Bolas thinks it misses the mark — easy to accidentally misplay and punishing when you do, yet with a low skill ceiling once learned, bucking Ret's beginner-friendly Dragonflight-era identity.
ptr.watch: Bolas's core worry is agency: faster, smoother generation plays better moment-to-moment than 12.0's spikiness, but it pushes Ret even further toward "dump Holy Power ASAP" with little management to optimize. Hero Talents remain Templar-dominant (though the new set is stronger for Herald of the Sun and balance has improved), held back partly by an Instrument of Retribution bug that makes Hammer of Wrath uncastable for the rest of an Avenging Wrath if a proc was already active — minor for Templar, a big loss for Herald of the Sun. Verdict call is editorial — draft.

ptr.theme: Void Shield centralization deepens instead of loosening
ptr.summary: Thread is appreciative that Blizzard engaged but frustrated the 12.1 pass introduces more problems than it solves: forcing a Void Shield proc via Mind Blast just raises its CPM rather than reducing how much the spec funnels through it, and pulling Atonement application off Penance adds ramp friction on top. The read is that Disc still functions almost entirely through Void Shield, and single-target healing stays weak despite the Shadow Mend buffs. Community wants a systemic redesign, not band-aids.
changes: Atonement no longer applied by Penance — ramp now leans harder on Power Word: Radiance and other appliers, adding friction
Void Shield proc reworked to a forced proc off Mind Blast; Clandon: this raises Void Shield CPM rather than reducing reliance
Clandon's Oracle raid example: Void Shield is ~25% of DPS, ~45% of direct healing, ~28% of Atonement healing, and 40%+ of total Atonement applications
Shadow Mend buffed for single-target healing but still stacks penalties (mana cost, cast time, talent investment) and is seen as weak
Greater Smite's 2s buff window and Power Word: Solace ('Uppies') on a 4-min CD both flagged as awkward/underwhelming

PTR build 68569 (2026-07-08): Void Shield absorption −30% with the power redistributed (all healing/absorption +10%, Ultimate Penitence +30%, Greater Smite 4s, Atonement 32% was 30%) — dev intent to reduce the overbearing focus on triggering Void Shield.
ptr.set2: Thread does not opine on the tier set; the feedback is confined to class-talent changes.
ptr.set4: Thread does not opine on the tier set, though its central complaint is directly relevant: a 4pc that buffs Void Shield absorption would reinforce exactly the Void Shield centralization the community is asking Blizzard to dial back.
ptr.watch: Whether the pass ships without numerically reducing Void Shield's throughput and redistributing that power — the thread's read is that until that happens, Disc keeps living and dying on Void Shield (Voidweaver hero tree, Entropic Rift, Inescapable Torment) with single-target healing left behind.

ptr.theme: Great spec changes, a placeholder tier set
ptr.summary: The fuller Wowhead review. Whispyr calls the spec changes genuinely great and well-executed — the reworked Implacable apex now refunds Energy on Envenom cast (no more intentionally letting the buff drop), a new Unstable Toxin capstone, talent repositioning, and Iron Wire now spreading make the rotation feel noticeably smoother with real build choices. But the patch is 'lacking otherwise': the tier set is another bland '10% Mastery' stat-stick that misses Blizzard's own complexity/impact goals, and the bigger pain points — Deathstalker's clunk and the dead bottom third of the class tree — went untouched.
changes: Implacable apex reworked (the headline change): Envenom +10% damage and now refunds 2 Energy per combo point on cast — no more waiting for the buff to drop. A welcome simplification; the S1 buff was notoriously buggy to track.
New capstone Unstable Toxin: Envenom +18% damage but -2s buff duration — a kiss-curse rewarding tight Envenom-window management, most practical in heavy AoE.

Iron Wire now spreads (via Crimson Tempest) and its stealth / Improved-Garrote Garrotes silence for 5s — new M+ utility vs a ~1-2% DPS choice. Internal Bleeding no longer spreads (self-cast Rupture/Kidney Shot; cover it with Canny Strikes).
Energy economy retuned: Dashing Scoundrel now grants baseline regen, Motivated Murderer 30% (was 20%), Blindside proc down — leaving Assa slightly faster, ~10-15% more Energy scaling into 5-8T AoE.

Bleed buffs (Shrouded Suffocation 30%, Avulsion 25%, Rapid Injection 20/40%) plus repositioning give a ~10% uplift in 2-4T cleave; Deathmark (75%, was 100%) and Kingsbane (-15%) nerfed to match the patch; +3% all damage baseline.
Class / hero: Thistle Tea is now a choice node (auto-at-low-Energy vs active-only), Atrophic Poison buffed to 4% / 60s; Fatebound's Deal Fate extra combo point cut to 60% (was 100%).
ptr.set2: A generic Nature-damage buff with no gameplay change and no build impact — Whispyr reads the whole set as another 'Mastery %' stat-stick in disguise.
ptr.set4: The bleed half of the same 'Mastery %' formula. Whispyr's big disappointment: blander than even the Season 1 set, it creates no gameplay and no build decisions and misses Blizzard's stated 'more complex / more impactful' goals — reads as a placeholder or in need of a full rework. Amplifying Poison stays the standard poison pick.
ptr.watch: Tuning isn't there yet: Unstable Toxin's -2s downside is currently 'actively crippling' in single target — it can cost more Envenom damage than the +18% adds and fights Rapid Injection's 40%, leaving you at the mercy of crit RNG; Iron Wire's new spread doesn't refresh on reapplication (possibly a bug). The louder critique is what went unaddressed: Deathstalker stays buggy, unintuitive and rarely played, and the bottom third of the class tree (Soothing Darkness / Subterfuge / Without a Trace, Thistle Tea, Cold Blooded Killer, Supercharger) stays bland — the obvious place to add the 'build options' Blizzard said they wanted.

Zero In (3%→2%) and Hidden Opportunity (Ambush proc 80%→100%) tweaks miss their stated mark.
ptr.set2: Dispatch damage +15% — does very little on its own.
ptr.set4: Sinister Strike / Ambush have a 12% chance to make the next Dispatch free at max combo points — adds a track-and-react hook and eases energy income. Currently bugged with Gravedigger, Hand of Fate coins, and Ruthlessness.
ptr.watch: Loss of the Precise Cuts era of cleave has turned Outlaw from a 2–4 target monster into "borderline unplayable" on those fights — and the upcoming raid has several 2–3 target encounters. Same trap hits all Rogues.

tierSet.set4: When Stormkeeper or Ascendance fade, your next 2 Lightning Bolts, Chain Lightnings, or Lava Bursts deal 25% increased damage and cause your next Earthquake, Earth Shock, or Elemental Blast to cost 100% less Maelstrom.

ptr.theme: Fun to play and buffed, but old structural baggage lingers
ptr.summary: Wordup's Wowhead review. The 12.1 changes are almost universally buffs (Totemic ~+3%, Stormbringer +3–5% by target count) and he strongly agrees the reworked spec's gameplay is 'very good'; the Season 2 tier set is a big step up from S1's whelming offering and looks like a solid all-rounder. But the article's weight is on 'baggage' that has lingered since Beta: an all-or-nothing cooldown profile on both hero trees, the frustrating Doom Winds/Ascendance 'deck of cards' RNG, lost priority/funnel damage, and Totemic slipping behind Stormbringer again — the same War Within reset pattern.
changes: Core buffs aimed at the weak downtime between cooldowns: Lightning Bolt & Chain Lightning +20%; Lava Lash & Stormstrike +15%; melee +15%.
Doom Winds now increases Windfury Weapon activation chance by 50% (was 100%) — trims the mass-AoE Windfury spike, felt mainly by Totemic.
Stormbringer nerf: Tempest main-target −10%, secondary-target −30%.
Totemic nerf: Surging Totem Tremor −15%, Surging Bolt −10%.

Net is still almost universally a buff (Totemic ~+3%, −1% in mass AoE; Stormbringer +3–5% by target count), but Wordup notes the devs' stated goal of shifting damage OUT of cooldown windows wasn't really achieved — only Totemic's Surging Totem took a real hit and it was mostly offset.

PTR build 68569 (2026-07-08): Venomous Abyss 4-set fixes — Short Circuit now actually gets consumed, Crash Lightning (Unleashed) again hits all targets, and the 4-set now boosts the Crash Lightning weapon-enhancement damage (averaged across active stacks).
ptr.set2: Reworked this week and much improved over S1. Built around Voltaic Blaze; the previous version had a single-target 'killswitch' (lost its 200% multiplier the moment a 2nd target appeared) — now a strong, self-sufficient universal bonus (its own Fire Nova procs) with the set's value weighted heavily here. Wordup is 'very happy' Blizzard caught the single-target-contingent design and pivoted before live.
ptr.set4: Adds extra Voltaic Blaze stacks plus Crash Lightning CDR. Heavily favors Stormbringer, which has the spare GCDs to weave Voltaic Blaze as a priority; Totemic struggles to juggle it against Storm Unleashed and its totem cadence. Fire Nova becomes a nice single-target pickup but is NOT a mandatory talent swap — a welcome, less-forceful tier-set design. Overall a solid all-rounder giving damage in nearly all situations.
ptr.watch: The 'baggage': both hero trees are all-or-nothing cooldown profiles with little build diversity ('a red Totemic and a blue Stormbringer'); the Doom Winds/Ascendance deck-of-cards RNG is a sore point — a 600-Maelstrom, 3-proc deck that can run ~1000 stacks (~5 min) between procs and doesn't reset without relogging, 'bordering on ruining the feel'; Descending Skies hard-locks Stormbringer to Ascendance with no controlled alternative; and the Crash Lightning damage split stripped Enhancement's traditional priority/funnel strength (Totemic hit hardest — Totemic Rebound fires on the wrong target). It's shaping up like the War Within pattern: Totemic is hit harder by the nerfs and gains less from the buffs/tier set while Stormbringer pulls ahead again. Structure/tuning, not resolved yet.

playstyle.notes: Bloodlust/Heroism, Skyfury raid buff, Capacitor/Earthgrab totems, Wind Shear interrupt, cleanses
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: 'a fairly steep learning curve', 'fast-paced', 'always be pressing something', many moving parts (Maelstrom Weapon stacks, cooldowns, procs) even after the Midnight reduction; Stormbringer build higher RNG and stricter windows.

ptr.theme: Only Farseer touched (Healing Rain rework)
ptr.summary: The only changes are to Farseer, via a Healing Rain rework; the other hero tree is untouched. The tier set is praised as thematic but is overshadowed, and the author flatly calls Resto Shaman the weakest raid healer in the game.
changes: Farseer Healing Rain reworked — the sole meaningful change.
The other hero tree gets nothing.
Baseline throughput problems remain unaddressed.
PTR build 68569 (2026-07-08): Acid Rain damage +50% but now ticks every 2s (was 1s); fixes so Healing Rain heals immediately on cast and both Acid/Healing Rain grant a proper final partial tick.
ptr.set2: Thematic and pleasant, but low-impact.
ptr.set4: Overshadowed by stronger healer sets this season.
ptr.watch: Author's verdict: weakest raid healer in the game right now. Baseline raid-B / M+-A — the M+ side carries it; the raid side is the concern the changes don't fix.

playstyle.notes: Bloodlust/Heroism, Skyfury, Spirit Link Totem, Wind Shear (only healer interrupt), cleanses, combat rez (self)
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: 'perhaps the easiest healer in the game' after Midnight removed ~half the active buttons; low skill floor, consistent healing, forgiving. Mastery is positioning/CD timing, not rotation.

tierSet.set4: Every 1 second, 1 ally inside of Healing Rain gains a watery shield that absorbs X damage for 10 seconds.

playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: 'straightforward and intuitive', embodies always-be-casting; simplified in Midnight (instant Call Dreadstalkers). Base rotation easy; Apex talent adds volatility/optimization depth. Moderate overall.

playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: 'a fairly straightforward spec', 'without much rotational complexity'; simple shard-generate-then-spend loop (Immolate + Chaos Bolt). One of the more accessible caster DPS.

tierSet.set4: Targets damaged by Echo of Sargeras take 6% increased damage from Echo of Sargeras for 6 seconds.

ptr.theme: A grab-bag rework — a couple wins, a couple losses, mostly mediocre
ptr.summary: Archimtiros reads the 12.1 Arms pass as "a couple good changes, a couple bad, and a whole lot of mediocre ones." Rend, Ravager, and Tactical Edge get real reworks, but losing guaranteed Sweeping Strikes uptime raises real questions about Arms as the "2-target pinnacle," and Colossus Smash alignment problems persist.
changes: Rend now costs 10 rage and is single-target only; Cleave applies it in AoE. Bloodletting grants 5% ability crit vs Rend targets but no longer extends Rend duration — cheaper, but refresh more often.
Ravager redesigned: +50% damage, no longer buffs Cleave/Whirlwind, and makes enemies take 25%→50% increased bleed damage for 12s; usable in single target again.
Broad Strokes cuts Sweeping Strikes to 6 charges (from 12) and Improved Sweeping Strikes is removed — no more 100% Sweep uptime.
Demolish moved to a flat 30s CD (was variable) but still won't align cleanly with Colossus Smash.
Tactical Edge redesigned so Colossus Smash grants a Sudden Death use; Master Tactician still terrible.

PTR build 68569 (2026-07-08): single-target buffs into Slayer — melee auto-attack +100%, the 2-set now boosts both Mortal Strike and Execute (was Mortal Strike only), and Slayer's Dominance proc chance 25% (was 15%); devs targeting Arms/Slayer's lagging single-target.
ptr.set4: Decent in AoE but terrible in single target, especially measured against S1's universally-powerful set — a real step down on the ST bench.
ptr.watch: Losing Improved Sweeping Strikes and 100% Sweep uptime puts Arms' claim as the 2-target pinnacle in question, and Demolish still won't line up with Colossus Smash — the alignment friction that dogged the spec hasn't been solved.
ptr.sourceLabel: Archimtiros (Icy Veins Arms author, SimC dev, Skyhold)

playstyle.notes: Battle Shout, Rallying Cry, Spell Reflection, Pummel interrupt, Piercing Howl; no rez/Lust/immunity
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: 'relatively simplistic with an easy to get into rotation', 'very straightforward and relatively simple'; beginner-friendly priority list. Depth added only by talent choices.

tierSet.set2: Mortal Strike damage increased by 10% and when you Slam a target, Slam also deals damage to enemies within 8 yards of it. Deals reduced damage beyond 5 targets.
tierSet.set4: Overpower damage increased by 15%. Mortal Strike and Overpower increase the damage of your next Slam by 10%, stacking up to 3 times.

ptr.theme: Good-on-paper changes that are hard to actually use — no real gameplay shift yet
ptr.summary: Archimtiros reads the 12.1 pass as generally good but hard to take advantage of, with no real gameplay changes landing right now. The bright spot is that the tweaks give hope Raging Blow builds might return, but as it stands the new talents largely function as point taxes and the payoff nodes hinge on Raging Blow being worth pressing over Bloodthirst, which it currently isn't.
changes: Rend simplified — no longer a standalone button, now applied by Thunder Clap / Whirlwind
Whirlwind now generates 3 Rage innately, opening up single-target use; new Carving Blades gives Whirlwind +50% damage on a single target (choice node vs. Meat Cleaver)
Rampaging Ruin redesigned to be more universal but plays as a talent-point tax — always taken in AoE, forcing you to drop Massacre / Executioner's Wrath / Deep Wounds / Cruelty
Hack and Slash: Rampage has a 75% chance to refund a Raging Blow charge — only matters if Raging Blow is worth using over Bloodthirst, which it currently isn't
Mountain Thane: Thunder Blast +40% but Crashing Thunder cut 30%→10%, making it more RNG-dependent
PTR build 68569 (2026-07-08): Bloodbath rebalanced toward the bleed — initial hit −12%, bleed +300% (dev-stated net-neutral to Bloodbath's overall damage).
ptr.set2: Reads weak. The Recklessness Raging Blow/Bloodthirst effects are each capped, blunting the payoff.
ptr.set4: Reads weak overall alongside the 2pc — the capped Recklessness effects leave the set feeling underwhelming.
ptr.watch: The whole Raging-Blow-refund payoff (Hack and Slash) is dead weight until Raging Blow becomes competitive with Bloodthirst; watch whether a later build shifts that balance, since it's the hinge for the Raging Blow build these changes are gesturing toward.
ptr.sourceLabel: Archimtiros (Icy Veins Fury author, SimC dev, Skyhold)

playstyle.notes: Battle Shout, Rallying Cry, Spell Reflection, Pummel interrupt, Piercing Howl; no rez/Lust/immunity
playstyle.complexityNotes: Icy Veins: 'relatively simple rotation' BUT 'one of the highest action specializations in the game'; high APM 'can be quite overwhelming while learning'. Simple logic, demanding execution — nudged to 3 for the APM burden.

tierSet.set2: Raging Blow damage increased by 15% and during Recklessness, Raging Blow extends the duration of Recklessness by 2 seconds, up to 6 seconds.
tierSet.set4: Bloodthirst damage increased by 10% and during Recklessness, Bloodthirst increases the critical strike bonus of Recklessness by 5%, up to 10%.

ptr.theme: Sensible defensive gains alongside a questionable bleed pivot; gameplay unchanged
ptr.summary: Mwahi/Archimtiros read it as a grab bag: some changes are good and make sense, others are really questionable, but gameplay-wise nothing actually changes. The defensive and Execute-support tweaks are welcome, while the Ravager bleed direction feels off-brand — "seems like we're being turned into a budget Assassination Rogue."
changes: Ignore Pain absorb +25% — great, especially for Colossus; Brutal Vitality 8%->10%
Devastating Focus and Tide of Battle now buff Execute too, so Execute finally hits for something (Colossus catch-up)
Ravager +50% damage, but the bleed focus is weird — 'budget Assassination Rogue' territory
Demolish moved to a fixed 30s CD: predictable ST burst, but ~10s longer effective CD in AoE (lost the Dominance of the Colossus reduction)
Mountain Thane: Thunder Blast +40%, but Crashing Thunder 30%->10% makes it more RNG-dependent
Revenge free-proc now also triggers from autos (was dodges/parries only) — shrinks the wild raid-vs-dungeon frequency gap

PTR build 68569 (2026-07-08): 4-set Bloody Rebuke bleed +100%, Unyielding's Defensive Stance bonus +6% (was 4%), and Colossus buffed (Practiced Strikes +4 Rage, No Stranger to Pain / Dominance of the Colossus improved) to close the gap with Mountain Thane.
ptr.set2: Lackluster — the free-Revenge proc is RNG-dependent, so it feels inconsistent.
ptr.set4: Insignificant on the bleed side, with some AoE potential at best.
ptr.watch: The Ravager/tier-set bleed pivot is the thing to watch — Archimtiros is skeptical it fits Prot's identity, and with the 2pc leaning on RNG procs the set may not move the needle even as Execute and defensives get healthier.
